_comment: SCUMM script patches applied at build time. Offsets are into the ORIGINAL extracted script file. Patches processed in order; file is rebuilt with length adjustments propagated. Each patch rewrites a print op ($14) that contains only a $0F text sub-op. Use \n to insert a SCUMM newline escape ($FF $01). Optional 'position' [x, y] injects a $00 pos sub-op before the text.

scripts/scrp_152_room010.bin._position_note: Default credit position now lives in the runtime (extractAndRenderString picks Y=168 when actor=$FF and dialogY==0 during a cutscene). Only explicit text replacements need patches here.

byte_patches._note: setCameraAt(480) -> setCameraAt(472). Shifts the LucasArts logo 8px right on the 256-wide SNES viewport so it centers cleanly. The shift MUST be a multiple of 8 so BG1HOFS stays tile-aligned — non-aligned offsets cause the tile cache to load the wrong palette for boundary tiles (the 'F' splotch and right-edge garbage we saw at cam=476).

CREDITS-VS-MUSIC SYNC (this + every patch below tagged 'sync'): the r010 title medley is non-looping and ends at var14=2977 (measured: theme starts at songBeatBase=5, 96.0s duration, credits-era play rate 30.0 ticks/s; early logo section runs faster so 5+96*30=2885 is WRONG — 2977 is the direct measurement). The vanilla schedule ran to var14=4343, i.e. ~46s of credits over silence. All var14 holds, beat targets, and page delays are scaled by (2977-5)/(4343-5)=0.685 so the credits end when the music does. sync: hold 250 -> 173
sync: final hold cap 5700 -> 2977 = music end. The hold only runs while isSoundRunning(theme) is true; the cap is the hard stop.
sync: delay 180 -> 103. Calibrated over two measured runs: end(T)=2827+13*(T-91) (all 13 delays execute); T=103 lands the credit end at var14~2983, ~0.2s after the music ends at 2977.

patches._note: Original had one 47-char line that wrapped past 32 cols; split to 3 lines. Drops the existing color=5 and SO_CENTER sub-ops (renderer already centers; white is fine).
